name: Anker PowerCore 20000 mAh Portable Charger
whatWorks: 20,000 mAh capacity is genuinely enough to power a modern smartphone five or six times over—enough for a 10–14 day trip without hunting for outlets at airports, hotels, or coffee shops
Anker's PowerIQ 2.0 automatically detects each device and delivers the optimal current, so phones, tablets, and earbuds all charge at their rated speed without requiring you to select the right port
Two simultaneous USB-A outputs let you charge a phone and tablet or two phones at the same time without splitting a single port's power budget

Anker's build quality and after-sale support are industry-leading at this price tier—the unit doesn't swell, doesn't get dangerously hot, and comes with an 18-month warranty backed by genuine customer service
worthKnowing: Uses Micro-USB for input (not USB-C), which means you'll need an adapter if your other devices are USB-C only; the cable is included but it's a legacy connector
No USB-C Power Delivery output—laptops requiring USB-C PD (like most MacBooks and many Chromebooks) won't charge from this unit
At 356g it's on the heavier side for carry-on travel; frequent travelers who prioritize weight savings might prefer a 10,000 mAh version at half the weight

commonComplaints.title: Micro-USB input feels outdated
commonComplaints.body: In 2025, nearly all cables are USB-C, and having to carry a Micro-USB cable just to recharge this power bank is a common frustration. Anker's newer PowerCore models have upgraded to USB-C input.

commonComplaints.title: No USB-C output
commonComplaints.body: Cannot charge USB-C laptops or tablets that require PD charging. For iPad Pro or laptop users who need more than phone-level charging, a USB-C PD power bank is required instead.

commonComplaints.title: Slow recharge of the bank itself
commonComplaints.body: Via Micro-USB, fully charging the 20,000 mAh bank takes 9–10 hours. Budget for an overnight top-up after each heavy-use day rather than expecting a quick recharge.

verdict: The Anker PowerCore 20000 remains one of the most reliable high-capacity power banks for travelers who primarily charge smartphones and earbuds. The PowerIQ charging, proven build quality, and Anker's reputation make it a safe purchase—and at $69 it's priced for value. The Micro-USB input is a legitimate dated drawback; if USB-C-first charging matters to you, look at Anker's newer 20K models with USB-C I/O. But for a traveler who wants to arrive with a charged phone no matter what, this delivers.

realBuyers.quote: Traveled through Southeast Asia for three weeks with this. Charged my iPhone and camera battery daily. Came back with charge still on the bank. Indispensable piece of kit.
The micro-USB input is annoying when everything else in my bag is USB-C, but the output speed and capacity are exactly what I need for long-haul flights.
Been using this for two years. No swelling, no heat issues, charges everything at full speed. Anker just works.

sources.title: Best Portable Chargers for Travel | Wirecutter

metaDescription: Anker PowerCore 20000 review: 20,000 mAh, charges iPhone 6× over, dual USB-A ports. Best high-capacity power bank for multi-day travel under $70 CAD.
introText: The Anker PowerCore 20000 (ASIN B06X9JCKR7) is a 20,000 mAh portable charger with dual USB-A outputs and PowerIQ 2.0 adaptive charging. Available on Amazon.ca, it provides approximately 6 iPhone charges or 5 Galaxy charges per cycle—ideal for multi-day trips without reliable outlet access.
